displayTitle: Aim Your Shielding, Then Swap the Source

beats.narration: Two tools shrink your dose on a gamma job: a collimator that aims your shielding, and a source changer for the riskiest swap you make.

payload.title: AIM IT, THEN SWAP IT
payload.sub: Collimators collapse the boundary. Changers keep the swap safe.

beats.narration: A collimator is tungsten you aim. It surrounds the exposure position and leaves open only the port the radiograph needs. The beam stays full strength.
Off-beam, the walls cut sixteen to over a hundredfold. Since rate falls with distance squared, a hundredfold pulls the shielded boundary in tenfold.

payload.heading: What collimation buys: the boundary collapses
payload.note: ÷100 OFF-BEAM → BOUNDARY PULLS IN ×10 · METER SETS THE ROPE

beats.narration: Iridium's seventy-four-day half-life means a fresh source roughly every quarter. The source changer is the shielded container built for that exchange.
For seconds in that swap, only procedure stands between you and a bare hundred-curie source. Survey, crank, lock, verify, survey again.

payload.sub: Crank feel and locks are never proof — only the meter is.
